name: Acolyte's Lenses
description:
You're pretty sure one of the Sahuagin Acolytes was holding a pair of these.
How they fit on your head is a mystery.

name: Agony's Eyes
description:
On bringing these goggles over your eyes, you see a flash of a memory: Wasting
away in the cold, then coming to in a Form distinctly unlike your own.

name: Alpha Phase Goggles
description: These goggles reveal certain things hidden on the ethereal plane.

name: Amethyst Loupe
description:
This single, jeweled Jewelers Loupe is able to magnify up to 100 times more
than your bare eye.
dropLocation: The Haunting of Saltmarsh, end chest

name: Arcane Goggles
description: This pair of goggles gives the wielder a remarkable insight into the arcane.

name: Archivist's Goggles
description: These goggles are said to allow the wielder to perceive arcane energies.

name: Attunement's Gaze
description: Gaze into the unknown.

name: Beta Phase Goggles
description: These goggles reveal certain things hidden on the ethereal plane.

name: Black Earth Guardian's Lenses
description: A guardian of the Black Earth is expected to be as stone. Become unbreakable.
dropLocation: ToEE: First Level and Earth Temple, end chest

name: Black Lace Blindfold
description:
A strange garment, perhaps meant to cover the eyes of the deceased. Perhaps
it was intended to be worn by a grieving widow.
dropLocation: The Mystery of Delera's Tomb, End chest

name: Blazing Sniper's Sights
description:
The Eternal Flame cannot be extinguished. Its flame grows with every invader
defeated.
dropLocation: ToEE: Lower Temple Complex, end chest

name: Blessed Goggles
description:
These enchanted goggles are said to grant a blessing upon those that gather
around the wearer.

name: Brightgaze
description:
Sight beyond sight, bright beyond bright. Bring light to your enemies, burn
back the night.
dropLocation: Peril of the Planar Eyes, any end chest

name: Cannith Crafted Goggles
description: This is a Cannith crafted set of goggles.

name: Cannoneer's Goggles
description:
Cannith safety guidelines recommend wearing these when operating any of their
ship-grade magical munitions equipment.
dropLocation: Precious Cargo, end chest; Pirates of the Thunder Sea (epic), quest chain end reward

name: Clear Lenses
description: This item cannot be fed to sentient jewels .

name: Collective Sight
description: You Make The 2019! Designed by the Dungeons and Dragons: Online Community.

name: Cormyrian Spectacles
description: This item cannot be fed to sentient jewels .

name: Corroded Iron Lenses
description: Salt and sea have done their work on these old frames.

name: Creation Forge Lenses
description:
Look out upon the world, and see the freedom that will one day belong to all
Warforged.

name: Desolation Spectacles
description:
The knowledge of Morgrave's library, defiled to a new purpose; what you can
see through them is power, and how to get it.
dropLocation: Unholy Defiler of the Hidden Hand, defiled version of Legendary Study Spectacles

name: Devoted Goggles
description: This pair of goggles will augment a cleric's ability to cast spells.

name: Discerning Gaze
description: Clear sight is often the key to victory.
dropLocation: Riding the Storm Out, Warded chest

name: Disordered Vision
description: This item cannot be fed to sentient jewels .

name: Doctor Gustav's Warped Lenses
description:
A set of black glasses that alter the vision. It makes a certain kind of sense,
but certainly not the standard kind.

name: Dragon Eyes
description:
"I look out upon the field of battle. What do my Dragon Eyes see?"
-Druth Felgon, Dragonborn Barbarian

name: Dream Visor (historic)
description:
The world as seen through this visor is a very strange place. People and things
are distorted and almost ethereal. Things that were previously hidden are now
visible.
dropLocation: The Prison of the Planes, Dal Quor room's chest

name: Dream Visor (Level 12)
description:
The world as seen through this visor is a very strange place. People and things
are distorted and almost ethereal. Things that were previously hidden are now
visible.
dropLocation: The Prison of the Planes, Dal Quor room's chest (Heroic Normal)

name: Dream Visor (Level 13)
description:
The world as seen through this visor is a very strange place. People and things
are distorted and almost ethereal. Things that were previously hidden are now
visible.
dropLocation: The Prison of the Planes, Dal Quor room's chest (Heroic Hard)

name: Dream Visor (Level 14)
description:
The world as seen through this visor is a very strange place. People and things
are distorted and almost ethereal. Things that were previously hidden are now
visible.
dropLocation: The Prison of the Planes, Dal Quor room's chest (Heroic Elite)

name: Dream Visor (Level 23)
description:
The world as seen through this visor is a very strange place. People and things
are distorted and almost ethereal. Things that were previously hidden are now
visible.
dropLocation: Return to Prison of the Planes, Dal Quor room's chest (Epic Normal)

name: Dream Visor (Level 24)
description:
The world as seen through this visor is a very strange place. People and things
are distorted and almost ethereal. Things that were previously hidden are now
visible.
dropLocation: Return to Prison of the Planes, Dal Quor room's chest (Epic Hard)

name: Dream Visor (Level 25)
description:
The world as seen through this visor is a very strange place. People and things
are distorted and almost ethereal. Things that were previously hidden are now
visible.
dropLocation: Return to Prison of the Planes, Dal Quor room's chest (Epic Elite)

name: Drow Smoke Goggles
description:
Smoke Goggles are a drow invention, lenses that have been darkened to allow
their drow wearers to tolerate the light of the sun while on surface raids.
This pair must have belonged to someone important, as it has been further enchanted
to help the wearer locate and strike vulnerable areas on their foes. However,
the darkly tinted lenses may somewhat hinder the normal vision of someone who
is already acclimated to sunlight.
dropLocation: The Battle for Eveningstar, end chest; Menace of the Underdark: The Darkening, quest chain end reward

name: Dusk Lenses
description: Peer into the gloom of the unknown.
dropLocation: Thralls of the Fungus Lord, End Chest

name: Electric Eyes
description:
Lightning courses through the rims of these goggles, highlighting sources of
energy for their wearer.

name: Epic Amethyst Loupe
description:
This single, jeweled Jewelers Loupe is able to magnify up to 100 times more
than your bare eye.

name: Epic Black Lace Blindfold
description:
A strange garment, perhaps meant to cover the eyes of the deceased. Perhaps
it was intended to be worn by a grieving widow.
dropLocation: The Mystery of Delera's Tomb, End Chest

name: Epic Corroded Iron Lenses
description: Salt and sea have done their work on these old frames.
dropLocation: Epic version of Corroded Iron Lenses

name: Epic Four Eyes
description: The Lord of Eyes sees all.
dropLocation: Terminal Delirium, second dance bonus chest

name: Epic Glimpse of the Soul
description: These goggles allow the wearer to see the true nature of someone's soul.
dropLocation: Inferno of the Damned (epic), End chest

name: Epic Goggles of the Inquisition
description:
These goggles are infused with the power of intimidation. This helps the Church
inquisitors to wring confessions from heretics and unbelievers.

name: Epic Goggles of Time-Sensing
description:
A strange, complex pair of goggles that allows the wearer to view the passage
of time more slowly than normal. Through this awareness the actions of the
wearer and his or her allies can occasionally be hastened.
dropLocation: Epic version of Goggles of Time-Sensing

name: Epic Kundarak Delving Goggles
description:
These goggles are Kundarak-forged and useful for exploring (and infiltrating)
secure spaces.
dropLocation: Epic version of Kundarak Delving Goggles

name: Epic Mentau's Goggles
description:
These goggles allow the wearer to see deep within the flesh of nearby foes allowing
blows to be targeted on the most vulnerable locations.

name: Epic Raven's Sight
description:
Goggles like these make the wearer's vision much sharper and are often employed
by drow scouts and spies.

name: Epic Sandstorm Glasses
description:
These thick-lensed goggles are made from canvas and cut amber crystals. They
enable the wearer to see in the most extreme conditions.

name: Epic Spectacles of Spirit Sight
description:
These spectacles enable the wearer to see the spiritual aura around all things.
This knowledge allows a spell caster to be more effective with spells that
affect the will or life force of the target.
dropLocation: Epic version of Spectacles of Spirit Sight

name: Eternal Flameguard's Lenses
description:
The Eternal Flame cannot be extinguished. Its flame grows with every invader
defeated.
dropLocation: ToEE: First Level and Earth Temple, end chest

name: Ethereal Gaze
description: These are slightly intangible.

name: Experimenter's Goggles
description: Tinker with the little things.

name: Eyes of Charming
description: Glass lenses that fit over your eyes.

name: Eyes of Defilement
description:
Absolute truth pales in comparison to making the impossible a reality. You can
see that now.
dropLocation: Unholy Defiler of the Hidden Hand, defiled version of Legendary Eyes of Enlightenment

name: Eyes of Enlightenment
description:
Once, an adventurer gazed upon absolute truth. They are long gone, but the lenses
they wore still remain.

name: Eyes of the Black Earth
description: The stone and causticity of the Black Earth, put to a more honorable purpose.
dropLocation: ToEE: Earth Node, end chest and random locked chest

name: Eyes of the Crushing Wave
dropLocation: ToEE: Water Node, end chest and random locked chest

name: Eyes of the Eternal Flame
description: The fires of the Eternal Flame, put to a more honorable purpose.
dropLocation: ToEE: Fire Node, end chest and random locked chest

name: Eyes of the Howling Hatred
description: The winds and weather of the Howling Hatred, put to a more honorable purpose.
dropLocation: ToEE: Air Node, end chest, Tempest 's chest, and random locked chest

name: Eyes of the Lady of Rot and Decay
dropLocation: ToEE: Depths of the Temple, end chest
_file: Eyes of the Lady of Rot and Decay.item

name: Feargaze
description: Looking through these goggles, the world seems just a little bit darker.

name: Four Eyes
description: The Lord of Eyes sees all.
dropLocation: Terminal Delirium, second dance bonus chest

name: Garstone's Lenses
description: These blast-grade lenses come with etched targeting sights.
dropLocation: Wrath of the Earth, End Chest

name: Glasses of the Meditative Mind
description: A deeply soothing sense sweeps over the wearer of these glasses.
dropLocation: Voices of the Dead, end chest

name: Glasses of the Night Brigade
description: The Night Brigade's finest lenses.
dropLocation: The Darkness and the Light, end chest

name: Glasses of the Studious Mage
description: Centuries of Elven Wizards used these lenses for deeper studies.

name: Glimpse of the Soul
description: These goggles allow the wearer to see the true nature of someone's soul.
dropLocation: Inferno of the Damned, end chest

name: Goggles of Dusk
description: The world through these goggles looks dead and decayed.
dropLocation: Army of Eternal Night, end chest

name: Goggles of Grabbing
description:
The Clan Tunnelworm Warden would sometimes use these goggles to halt a potential
prisoner in their tracks, simply by staring them down.

name: Goggles of Insight
description:
These goggles are fitted with lenses that allow the user to perceive weaknesses
in any opponent's armor.

name: Goggles of Intuition
description:
Anyone who wears these goggles is able to quickly identify weak points and vulnerable
spots for a physical blow.

name: Goggles of Perception
description: These goggles sharpen its wearer's eyes, giving a bonus on search and spot skills.

name: Goggles of Shielding
description: Anyone who wears these goggles feels as if they are protected by an unseen force.

name: Goggles of the Archer
description: Archers of ages past used these goggles to improve their accuracy.

name: Goggles of the Berserker
description: Red lenses adorn these goggles made for those already used to seeing red.
dropLocation: Secrets of the Red Wizards, end chest

name: Goggles of the Frozen Night
description: A mild warmth eminates around the lenses, preventing frost from forming.

name: Goggles of the Inquisition
description:
These goggles are infused with the power of intimidation. This helps the Church
inquisitors to wring confessions from heretics and unbelievers.

name: Goggles of the Warrior
description: These lenses grant sight to those who would wage war for a just cause.

name: Goggles of Time-Sensing
description:
A strange, complex pair of goggles that allows the wearer to view the passage
of time more slowly than normal. Through this awareness the actions of the
wearer and his or her allies can occasionally be hastened.

name: Goggles of Unbroken Sight
description: These are magical goggles that will prevent you from becoming blinded.

name: Green Steel Goggles
description: Green steel forms the frame of these goggles.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Guardian's Glasses (Level 19)
description: This item cannot be fed to sentient jewels .
dropLocation: A Break In the Ice, End reward

name: Guardian's Glasses (Level 27)
description: This item cannot be fed to sentient jewels .
dropLocation: A Break In the Ice, End reward

name: Intricate Field Optics (historic)
description:
These field optics are of too precise a workmanship for the ancient giants to
have created them. They must have been assembled by elven artisans in the presence
of a giant master.

name: Intricate Field Optics (Level 12)
description:
These field optics are of too precise a workmanship for the ancient giants to
have created them. They must have been assembled by elven artisans in the presence
of a giant master.
dropLocation: Gianthold Tor, White Dragon 's chest (Heroic Normal)

name: Intricate Field Optics (Level 13)
description:
These field optics are of too precise a workmanship for the ancient giants to
have created them. They must have been assembled by elven artisans in the presence
of a giant master.
dropLocation: Gianthold Tor, White Dragon 's chest (Heroic Hard)

name: Intricate Field Optics (Level 14)
description:
These field optics are of too precise a workmanship for the ancient giants to
have created them. They must have been assembled by elven artisans in the presence
of a giant master.
dropLocation: Gianthold Tor, White Dragon 's chest (Heroic Elite)

description:
These field optics are of too precise a workmanship for the ancient giants to
have created them. They must have been assembled by elven artisans in the presence
of a giant master.
dropLocation: Return to Gianthold Tor, White Dragon 's chest (Epic Normal)

description:
These field optics are of too precise a workmanship for the ancient giants to
have created them. They must have been assembled by elven artisans in the presence

dropLocation: Return to Gianthold Tor, White Dragon 's chest (Epic Hard)

description:
These field optics are of too precise a workmanship for the ancient giants to
have created them. They must have been assembled by elven artisans in the presence
of a giant master.
dropLocation: Return to Gianthold Tor, White Dragon 's chest (Epic Elite)

name: Intricate Lenses
description: This item cannot be fed to sentient jewels .

name: Ir'Kesslan's Most Prescient Lens
description:
This lens was crafted for King Sebastes ir'Kesslan, but has fallen into disrepair
after its sudden disappearance.
dropLocation: Too Hot to Handle, End chest; Masterminds of Sharn (Epic) end reward; also rare drop in any Sharn quest

name: Jeweler's Loupe
description:
Magnifying lenses allow a skilled appraiser to view gems and other valuables
with great clarity.
dropLocation: Cult of the Six, End reward

name: Karissa's Goggles
description:
The goggles of Karissa the Knife, Master Thief of the Night Brigade, house a
small switch that allows the user to darken the lenses.

name: Kundarak Delving Goggles
description:
These goggles are Kundarak-forged and useful for exploring (and infiltrating)
secure spaces.
dropLocation: Plane of Night, Raid Warded chest

description:
You're pretty sure one of the Sahuagin Acolytes was holding a pair of these.
How they fit on your head is a mystery.

description:
On bringing these goggles over your eyes, you see a flash of a memory: Wasting
away in the cold, then coming to in a Form distinctly unlike your own.

This single, jeweled Jewelers Loupe is able to magnify up to 100 times more
than your bare eye.
dropLocation: The Haunting of Saltmarsh, end chest, or Legendary version of Epic Amethyst Loupe

dropLocation: ToEE: First Level and Earth Temple, end chest

A set of black glasses that alter the vision. It makes a certain kind of sense,

name: Legendary Eyes of the Lady of Rot and Decay
description:
All is entropy, all is destined for decay. Her last hope is that you might see
everything as she does.
dropLocation: ToEE: Depths of the Temple, end chest

description:
A strange, complex pair of goggles that allows the wearer to view the passage
of time more slowly than normal. Through this awareness the actions of the
wearer and his or her allies can occasionally be hastened.
dropLocation: Legendary version of Epic Goggles of Time-Sensing

name: Legendary Green Steel Goggles
description: Green steel forms the frame of these goggles.
dropLocation: Altar of Fecundity, Manufactured Ingredient Recipes

name: Legendary Karissa's Goggles
description:
The goggles of Karissa the Knife, Master Thief of the Night Brigade, house a
small switch that allows the user to darken the lenses.

name: Legendary Kundarak Delving Goggles
description:
These goggles are Kundarak-forged and useful for exploring (and infiltrating)
secure spaces.
dropLocation: Legendary version of Epic Kundarak Delving Goggles

name: Legendary Lenses of the Dark Depths
description: Peer into the depths below...
_file: Legendary Lenses of the Dark Depths.item

name: Legendary Lenses of the Orator
description:
The fine lenses of these goggles were once part of a gem held by Kor Kaza, imbued
with potent persuasive power.

name: Legendary Magewright's Spectacles
description:
A pair of spectacles often worn by House Cannith magewrights. It enhances the
functions of one's brain, improving the ability of the wearer to focus his
or her energies.
dropLocation: Legendary Master Artificer, End and Optional Chest

name: Legendary Mining Lenses
description: While wearing these, rocks and debris in your way become simply a miner inconvenience.

name: Legendary Mystic Monocle
description:
You may see your foes, but can you see the way to their core? Their very soul?
It was once said this monocle let its user see both.

name: Legendary Negotiator's Spectacles
description:
Even in a dismal place like Barovia, the right pair of spectacles will lend
credence to your debating points.

name: Legendary Paramnesial Lenses
description:
Enchanted heavily with illusionary magic, these goggles sometimes make it hard
to discern the reality around you.
dropLocation: Memoirs of an Illusory Larcener, End Chest

name: Legendary Precision Lenses
description: Deadly and efficient.
dropLocation: White Plume Mountain, End chest, Burket and Snarla's chest

name: Legendary Raven's Sight
description:
Goggles like these make the wearer's vision much sharper and are often employed
by drow scouts and spies.
dropLocation: Legendary version of Epic Raven's Sight

name: Legendary Sandstorm Glasses
description:
These thick-lensed goggles are made from canvas and cut amber crystals. They
enable the wearer to see in the most extreme conditions.
dropLocation: Legendary version of Epic Sandstorm Glasses

name: Legendary Scientist's Sights
description:
These lenses slightly magnify small details, a fine bit of magic for the aspiring
tinkerer.

name: Legendary Sight of the Sleepwalker
description: These will allow you to see beyond the world that you inhabit.

name: Legendary Spectacles of Spirit Sight
description:
These spectacles enable the wearer to see the spiritual aura around all things.
This knowledge allows a spell caster to be more effective with spells that
affect the will or life force of the target.
dropLocation: Legendary version of Epic Spectacles of Spirit Sight

name: Legendary Study Spectacles
description:
A pair of spectacles that fell behind a heavy shelf in the library. Years of
exposure to the library's knowledge have permanently changed them.
dropLocation: The Devils to Pay, End chest

name: Legendary Summoner's Spectacles
description:
These finely-crafted glasses are best used in tandem with a large and terrifying
wolf.

name: Legendary Symphonic Lenses
description:
Cracked but not yet broken, these spectacles once belonged to a brilliant Barovian
composer who spent his life writing organ music. After creative differences
with Strahd himself, his career was ended early - and quite suddenly.
dropLocation: An Invitation to Dinner, End chest, Challenge Strahd Again optional chest

name: Legendary Tharne's Goggles
description:
This pair of goggles was discovered by an infamous dungeon delver by the name
of Tharne, during one of adventures in the ruins beneath Stormreach. Apparently
his latest adventure did not go as well as previous exploits. There is said
to be a matching set of bracers.
dropLocation: Legendary Vision of Destruction, End chest

name: Legendary Tinkerer's Goggles
description:
Goggles worn by House Cannith tinkers to enhance their vision, aiding them in
working with small or intricate mechanisms.
dropLocation: Legendary Master Artificer, End and Optional chest

name: Legendary Viktra's Experiment
description:
Crafted by Viktra, these goggles did not prove out a hypothesis and were immediately
discard. In the right hands, though, they may find new purpose.

name: Lenses of Doom
description:
The lenses of these goggles where made from the retinas of a pair of Cholthulzz's
eyes
dropLocation: Litany of the Dead, Cholthulzz 's chest

name: Lenses of Opportunity
description:
A valuable treasure of the servants of the Lord of Eyes. These goggles are extremely
sensitive and will point out an enemy's every weakness, allowing you to strike
where the most damage will be dealt. however, you are likely to miss the details
of your environment while wearing them.

name: Lenses of Peering
description: These fine lenses can help you spot and search for hidden things.

name: Lenses of the Dark Depths
description: Peer into the depths below...

name: Lenses of the Orator
description:
The fine lenses of these goggles were once part of a gem held by Kor Kaza, imbued
with potent persuasive power.

name: Lenses of the Woodsman
description: These lenses have been enchanted to allow sight in even the darkest conditions.
dropLocation: Eveningstar, Turn in 5 Commendations: Druids of the King's Forest Commendation: Druids of the King's Forest : This is a valuable item that can be exchanged for goods if brought to a Challenge Trader. This is a token of favor earned by helping the Druids of the King's Forest. You can exchange it in Eveningstar for powerful equipment.

name: Magewright's Spectacles
description:
A pair of spectacles often worn by House Cannith magewrights. It enhances the
functions of one's brain, improving the ability of the wearer to focus his
or her energies.
dropLocation: House Cannith Manufactury, Stellite 's Chest (Rare encounter)

name: Magnifying Lens
description:
This singular lens will give you a fine advantage versus even the most deadly
of traps.

name: Malchor's Undersun Goggles
description:
Created by the brilliant wizard Malchor Harpell, these goggles have been enchanted
to allow the wearer an approximation of sight while venturing into the Underdark
. Unfortunately, this magical vision is imperfect, and can be somewhat jarring.

name: Mentau's Goggles
description:
These goggles allow the wearer to see deep within the flesh of nearby foes allowing
blows to be targeted on the most vulnerable locations.
dropLocation: Litany of the Dead, Mentau 's chest

name: Mining Lenses
description: While wearing these, rocks and debris in your way become simply a miner inconvenience.

name: Misery Monocle
description:
You've seen what lies at the hearts of those who oppose you; now you see how
to break them.
dropLocation: Unholy Defiler of the Hidden Hand, defiled version of Legendary Mystic Monocle

name: Mystic Monocle
description:
You may see your foes, but can you see the way to their core? Their very soul?
It was once said this monocle let its user see both.

name: Negotiator's Spectacles
description:
Even in a dismal place like Barovia, the right pair of spectacles will lend
credence to your debating points.

name: Ornate Goggles
description: These goggles are fitted with magnifying lenses, aiding the wearer's perception.

name: Paramnesial Lenses
description:
Enchanted heavily with illusionary magic, these goggles sometimes make it hard
to discern the reality around you.
dropLocation: Memoirs of an Illusory Larcener, End Chest

name: Perfected Goggles of Time-Sensing
description:
A strange, complex pair of goggles that allows the wearer to view the passage
of time more slowly than normal. Through this awareness the actions of the
wearer and his or her allies can occasionally be hastened.

name: Precision Lenses
description: Deadly and efficient.

name: Raven's Sight
description:
Goggles like these make the wearer's vision much sharper and are often employed
by drow scouts and spies.

name: Razorclaw's Lenses
description: Knocked a little loose in the brawl, but they still work well enough.

name: Red Wizard's Sight
description: A vision for the future.
dropLocation: Threats Old and New, Raid Warded chest

name: Rose Colored Goggles
description:
Feeling nostalgic? Put these on to bring yourself back to 2006, where things
were so much simpler.

name: Sage's Spectacles (Level 15)
description: This item cannot be fed to sentient jewels .
dropLocation: A Break In the Ice, End reward

name: Sage's Spectacles (Level 27)
description: This item cannot be fed to sentient jewels .
dropLocation: A Break In the Ice, End reward

name: Sandstorm Glasses
description:
These thick-lensed goggles are made from canvas and cut amber crystals. They
enable the wearer to see in the most extreme conditions.

name: Scientist's Sights
description:
These lenses slightly magnify small details, a fine bit of magic for the aspiring
tinkerer.

name: Scoundrel's Lenses
description: Knocked a little loose in the brawl, but they still work well enough.

name: Shaded Lenses
description: This item cannot be fed to sentient jewels .

name: Shadow Sight
description: Night can be an ally to those who remain unhindered by her shadow.

name: Shadowsight (Level 14)
description:
Goggle attuned to the Shadowfell that makes hitting shadows and other incorporeal
creatures easier.
dropLocation: Through a Mirror Darkly, end chest (Heroic Normal); Shadow Over Wheloon, quest chain end reward (Heroic)

name: Shadowsight (Level 15)
description:
Goggle attuned to the Shadowfell that makes hitting shadows and other incorporeal
creatures easier.
dropLocation: Through a Mirror Darkly, end chest (Heroic Hard)

name: Shadowsight (Level 16)
description:
Goggle attuned to the Shadowfell that makes hitting shadows and other incorporeal
creatures easier.
dropLocation: Through a Mirror Darkly, end chest (Heroic Elite)

name: Shadowsight (Level 25)
description:
Goggle attuned to the Shadowfell that makes hitting shadows and other incorporeal
creatures easier.
dropLocation: Through a Mirror Darkly, end chest (Epic Normal); Shadow Over Wheloon, quest chain end reward (Heroic)

name: Shadowsight (Level 26)
description:
Goggle attuned to the Shadowfell that makes hitting shadows and other incorporeal
creatures easier.
dropLocation: Through a Mirror Darkly, end chest (Epic Hard)

name: Shadowsight (Level 27)
description:
Goggle attuned to the Shadowfell that makes hitting shadows and other incorporeal
creatures easier.
dropLocation: Through a Mirror Darkly, end chest (Epic Elite)

name: Sight of the Sleepwalker
description: These will allow you to see beyond the world that you inhabit.

name: Simple Spectacles
description: This item cannot be fed to sentient jewels .

name: Skirmisher's Lenses (Level 19)
description: This item cannot be fed to sentient jewels .
dropLocation: A Break In the Ice, End reward

name: Skirmisher's Lenses (Level 27)
description: This item cannot be fed to sentient jewels .
dropLocation: A Break In the Ice, End reward

name: Spectacles of Spirit Sight
description:
These spectacles enable the wearer to see the spiritual aura around all things.
This knowledge allows a spell caster to be more effective with spells that
affect the will or life force of the target.
dropLocation: The Chamber of Raiyum, Treasure Room Chest

name: Spectacular Optics
description:
These lenses are enchanted to let their wearer glimpse a second into the future,
allowing them to avoid traps and attacks.

name: Steps' Lenses of Seein' Hidden Hand Secrets
description: This item cannot be fed to sentient jewels .
dropLocation: Steps Obliss, Turn in Seeing the Impossible: Behind the Dark Curtain
_file: Steps' Lenses of Seein' Hidden Hand Secrets.item

name: Study Spectacles
description:
A pair of spectacles that fell behind a heavy shelf in the library. Years of
exposure to the library's knowledge have permanently changed them.
dropLocation: The Devils to Pay, End chest

name: Summoner's Spectacles
description:
These finely-crafted glasses are best used in tandem with a large and terrifying
wolf.

name: Symphonic Lenses
description:
Cracked but not yet broken, these spectacles once belonged to a brilliant Barovian
composer who spent his life writing organ music. After creative differences
with Strahd himself, his career was ended early - and quite suddenly.
dropLocation: An Invitation to Dinner, End chest, Challenge Strahd Again optional chest

name: Syne, the Way of the Melody
description: TBD
_file: Syne, the Way of the Melody.item

name: Tharne's Goggles
description:
This pair of goggles was discovered by an infamous dungeon delver by the name
of Tharne, during one of adventures in the ruins beneath Stormreach. Apparently
his latest adventure did not go as well as previous exploits. There is said
to be a matching set of bracers.
dropLocation: A Vision of Destruction, End chest

name: The Masque
description: A broken fragment of a mask. Oddly, it's warm to the touch.
dropLocation: The Curse of Strahd, warded chest

name: Tinker's Goggles
description:
Goggles worn by House Cannith tinkers to enhance their vision, aiding them in
working with small or intricate mechanisms.
dropLocation: House Cannith Manufactury, Bulat 's chest (Rare encounter) ; Schemes of the Enemy, Dackle's chest (only on Normal difficulty)

name: Trapblast Goggles
description:
The Hobgoblins of Cloven Jaw often used these goggles when setting their deadly
traps to pre-emptively protect themselves from misfire.
dropLocation: The Seal of Shan-To-Kor, End reward

name: Troubleshooter's Goggles
description: This pair of goggles will increase your awareness of the surrounding area.

name: Van Richten's Spectacles
description:
Lost in an earlier age, these enchanted spectacles hone one's eyes to see the
most minor of details.

name: Viktra's Experiment
description:
Crafted by Viktra, these goggles did not prove out a hypothesis and were immediately
discard. In the right hands, though, they may find new purpose.

name: Visions of Precision
description:
These lenses were crafted to allow their bearers greater focus on the battlefield;
they seem to filter out anything that would distract from one's goals.
dropLocation: Defiler of the Just, warded chest

name: Visor of Concentration
description:
Some mystics believe that by weakening one sense, another is sharpened. This
enchanted visor is based around this belief.

name: Visor of the Flesh Render Guards
description:
This visor was acquired from the hobgoblin guard that watches over the trapped
flesh render in the depths of Splinterskull.
_file: Visor of the Flesh Render Guards.item

name: Wolfinson's Monocular Enhancer
description:
A strange, singular, glass eyepiece that appear to be a relic from the Last
War. Legend has it that Professor Philius Wolfinson used it to repair constructs
during that time period, until it was lost on an expedition to Xen'drik to
recover ancient artifacts for House Cannith.
